# 3PPS.info

> Educational resource for USPTO Third-Party Preissuance Submissions under 35 U.S.C. § 122(e)

## What This Site Covers

3PPS.info explains the Third-Party Preissuance Submission (3PPS) — the statutory mechanism that allows any member of the public to submit prior art to a USPTO patent examiner before a patent is granted. The site is written for non-practitioners: engineers, founders, product managers, and anyone facing a patent application that may affect their work.

Key topics covered:
- What a 3PPS is and how it differs from a pre-grant opposition (it is not an opposition)
- The statutory filing window and deadline calculation (under 35 U.S.C. § 122(e) and 37 CFR 1.290)
- How to write compliant Concise Descriptions of Relevance (factual only — no legal arguments)
- When filing a 3PPS can backfire versus when to hold prior art for an Inter Partes Review (IPR)
- How the US 3PPS compares to Third-Party Observations at the EPO and under the PCT
- Filing costs: free for first-time filers submitting 3 or fewer documents; $180 for subsequent filings

## Key Facts

- Anyone can file a 3PPS — no attorney required
- Filing can be done anonymously under 37 CFR 1.290
- The filing window closes when a Notice of Allowance (NOA) issues — with no warning
- Permitted prior art types: patents (US and foreign), published patent applications, printed publications
- Not permitted: declarations, affidavits, legal arguments, obviousness conclusions
- After filing, the submitter's role ends completely — no participation in proceedings

## Pages

- [3PPS 101: The Workflow](https://3pps.info/101/) — 4-step process guide
- [Deadline Calculator](https://3pps.info/calculator/) — statutory window calculator
- [Drafting Guide](https://3pps.info/drafting/) — how to write compliant descriptions
- [Strategic Risks](https://3pps.info/strategy/) — when 3PPS backfires vs. holding art for IPR
- [Filing Checklist](https://3pps.info/checklist/) — interactive pre-flight checklist
- [International Comparison](https://3pps.info/international/) — US vs. EPO vs. PCT
- [FAQ](https://3pps.info/faq/) — common questions answered
- [Glossary](https://3pps.info/glossary/) — key patent law terms in plain English
- [About](https://3pps.info/about/) — about this site and its author

## Author

Built by Kieran O'Leary. See https://kieranjoleary.com for more.

## Governing Law

- 35 U.S.C. § 122(e) — statutory authority
- 37 CFR 1.290 — implementing regulation
- America Invents Act (AIA), signed 2011

## Disclaimer

This site is educational only and is not affiliated with the USPTO. Nothing here constitutes legal advice.
